pattern: Each row of a 3-cell strip has exactly two valid coloring patterns (type-121 and type-123); track their counts row by row using fixed transition multipliers, then multiply by constants per row transition.
hints: Classify each valid row coloring into two shapes: alternating (a-b-a) or all-different (a-b-c); count each type separately.
A type-121 row can transition to 3 type-121 rows and 2 type-123 rows; a type-123 row can transition to 2 type-121 rows and 2 type-123 rows.
Iterate n times updating the count of each type; the answer is the sum of both counts modulo 10^9+7.
